purpose: Reviewed descriptive inventory of public pipeline YAML steps. This registry is not the runtime source of truth.

entries.name: identity
entries.purpose: Return the pipeline payload unchanged.

inverse.reason: Pass-through operation.

entries.name: validate
entries.purpose: Validate duplicate IDs and unresolved FK references with severity policy.

inverse.reason: Validation does not transform owned data.

entries.name: add_fk_helpers
entries.purpose: Materialize FK helper columns from detected FK conventions and configured helper fields.

defaults.notes: May include helper_value_mode with canonical values values or formula; singular aliases value and formulas are accepted by the current runtime.

writes.reason: Derived helper provenance is a transient current-run payload registered in meta_registry.yaml.

inverse.reason: Helper columns are derived projections from referenced data.

entries.name: remove_fk_helpers
entries.purpose: Remove materialized FK helper columns using derived provenance when available and prefix fallback otherwise.

reads.reason: Derived helper provenance is a transient current-run payload registered in meta_registry.yaml.

writes.reason: Cleanup synchronizes the transient derived helper provenance payload.

inverse.reason: Cleanup removes derived helper projections and cannot reconstruct them without source policy.

entries.name: validate_fk_helpers
entries.purpose: Validate FK helper consistency without changing frames.

inverse.reason: Validation does not transform owned data.

entries.name: plugin
entries.purpose: Run a caller-provided dotted callable as an explicit pipeline extension point.

inverse.reason: Plugin behavior is owned by the external callable.

entries.name: flatten_headers
entries.purpose: Flatten MultiIndex or tuple-like DataFrame headers into scalar labels.

inverse.reason: Reversibility depends on separator choice and header value ambiguity.

entries.name: unflatten_headers
entries.purpose: Split scalar string headers into MultiIndex headers.

inverse.reason: Reversibility depends on separator choice and header value ambiguity.

entries.name: reorder_fk_helpers
entries.purpose: Move FK helper columns next to their FK column for spreadsheet readability.

inverse.reason: Column order changes, but row values are preserved.

entries.name: add_validations
entries.purpose: Write validation constraints into workbook metadata for later rendering.

inverse.reason: Writes validation intent and does not own data transformation.

entries.name: validate_references
entries.purpose: Validate declarative primary-key, uniqueness, foreign-key, and reference-tuple integrity rules against concrete frame data.

rules.notes: Rule mappings may include validation gating via row-local `when` or global `enabled_when`; this does not control pipeline flow.

replaces: findings frame when present in warn mode

inverse.reason: Checks frame data and optionally emits a findings report; it does not transform canonical data.

entries.name: validate_graph
entries.purpose: Validate explicitly configured node/edge networks for endpoint existence and duplicate edge identities.

replaces: findings frame when present in warn mode

inverse.reason: Checks graph-shaped frame relationships and optionally emits a findings report; it does not transform canonical data.

entries.name: configure_workbook_view
entries.purpose: Declare which already prepared frames render as workbook sheets, including sheet names, sheet order, lifecycle annotations, and per-sheet rendering options.

sheets.notes: Sheet specs may include sheet-local helper_columns style metadata; data transforms such as select, filter, join, and pivot remain separate preceding steps.

inverse.reason: Workbook view configuration does not transform data; it controls render-time sheet selection and metadata.

entries.name: apply_workbook_view_sheet_mappings
entries.purpose: Re-key visible workbook sheets to logical workbook-view frames on readback using the persisted _meta.workbook_view.sheet_mappings, without inferring from sheet names, order, labels, or columns.

logical_frames.notes: Validation boundary only: full allowed set of referenced logical frame identities. Must include both mapping.logical_frame and optional mapping.canonical_frame when present; not a selection or inference heuristic.

drops: original visible workbook sheet frame keys after remap

inverse.reason: Forward sheet naming is owned by configure_workbook_view; this readback step relabels visible sheets to logical frames using the authoritative persisted mapping and changes no rows or columns.

entries.name: configure_lookup_helpers
entries.purpose: Resolve lookup-helper policy and persist it as canonical helper policy metadata.

inverse.reason: Writes resolved helper policy metadata and does not own data transformation.

entries.name: configure_fk_helpers
entries.purpose: Resolve explicit/manual FK-helper target policies and persist them as canonical v1 helper policy metadata for current FK-helper primitives. Additionally writes v2 relation entries through a transitional v1->v2 bridge that scans current data frames for the configured literal fk_column header (one v2 entry per source frame carrying that header). Heuristic relation inference belongs to infer_fk_relations. The bridge is current-state compatibility only; FTR-FK-HELPERS-POLICY-DRIVEN-PRIMITIVES-P5 owns narrowing or removing it once primitives consume v2 directly.

targets.notes: Public YAML form. Mapping keyed by FK target sheet/key with per-target configuration. The 'auto' heuristic mode is no longer accepted; use the infer_fk_relations step instead. The domain callable also supports a single-target argument, but the pipeline binding reserves target for the callable address.

auto.notes: Default policy options (helper_candidates, default_helpers.prefer, fk_column_pattern) applied as fallbacks to explicit per-target configuration. Does not enable inference.

writes.notes: v1 per-target shape, consumed by current FK-helper primitives. Retained only for current primitive consumers; narrowed or removed by FTR-FK-HELPERS-POLICY-DRIVEN-PRIMITIVES-P5.

v2 relation entries, derived by transitional v1->v2 bridge: one entry per (source_frame, source_column) pair whose source frame carries the resolved literal fk_column. Transitional compatibility for the period in which configuration steps produce v2 but primitives still consume v1.
v2 marker (currently 2). Co-exists with v1 per-target keys under the same root during the transition; v1+v2 coexistence is not a permanent dual authority.

inverse.reason: Writes resolved helper policy metadata and does not own data transformation.

entries.name: infer_fk_relations
entries.purpose: Infer FK relation policy via bounded heuristics over current data frames and persist v2 relation entries under helper_policies.fk. Writes the v2 producer shape only; does not write the v1 per-target shape, so current FK-helper primitives do not pick up inferred relations until FTR-FK-HELPERS-POLICY-DRIVEN-PRIMITIVES-P5 makes them v2-aware.

id_columns.notes: Candidate primary-key column names on target frames. Default ['id'].

fk_patterns.notes: FK column header patterns; each must contain the placeholder '{target}'. Default ['id_({target})'].

target_label_fields.notes: Ordered candidate display fields on target frames; matched fields become helper_fields. Default ['name', 'label'].

on_ambiguous.notes: Policy when a single FK column matches multiple candidate target frames. One of 'fail' (default) or 'ignore'.

on_missing_target.notes: Policy when an FK column matches no candidate target frame or the target has no recognized id column. One of 'fail' (default) or 'ignore'.

writes.notes: v2 relation entries with produced_by.step=infer_fk_relations. apply_v2_relations enforces conflict rules: cross-producer collisions on the same (source_frame, source_column) key fail; same-producer rewrites are accepted only when semantically identical.
v2 marker (currently 2). v1+v2 coexistence is transitional; FTR-FK-HELPERS-POLICY-DRIVEN-PRIMITIVES-P5 owns narrowing or removing the v1 retention.

inverse.reason: Writes resolved FK relation policy metadata and does not own data transformation.

entries.name: bootstrap_meta
entries.purpose: Merge profile defaults, persisted metadata, and CLI/runtime overrides into the payload metadata.

reads.reason: Bootstrap may merge arbitrary caller-supplied registered metadata.
reads.review_condition: Review whenever bootstrap_meta introduces a concrete persistent metadata root rather than forwarding registered caller metadata.

writes.reason: Bootstrap may write arbitrary caller-supplied registered metadata.
writes.review_condition: Review whenever bootstrap_meta introduces a concrete persistent metadata root rather than forwarding registered caller metadata.

inverse.reason: Metadata merge precedence is not an invertible data operation.

entries.name: apply_overrides
entries.purpose: Load or apply workbook and sheet override metadata.

inverse.reason: Override merge precedence is not an invertible data operation.

entries.name: write_structured_yaml
entries.purpose: Write explicit nested YAML configuration files from tabular frames and record a generated-file report frame.

inverse.reason: The writer emits configured projections to files; reverse import of those files is outside this FTR.

entries.name: write_key_value_resources
entries.purpose: Write deterministic key-value (.properties-style) files from a configured source frame, partitioned by template column values, and record a generated-file report frame.

inverse.reason: The writer serializes frame data to files; reimporting those files is outside this FTR.

entries.name: write_artifact_manifest
entries.purpose: Merge writer report frames into a deterministic shared-shape manifest frame; optionally compute SHA-256 checksums and write a YAML or JSON manifest artifact.

inverse.reason: The manifest step merges and summarizes writer reports; it does not produce reversible file data.

entries.name: apply_derived_column_policy
entries.purpose: Drop helper/derived columns from a payload frame using only registered _meta.derived provenance and optionally value-check enrich_lookup helpers against their source frame.

reads.reason: Reads registered FK helper-column provenance to identify columns to drop on reimport.
Reads registered enrich_lookup helper provenance to identify and value-check lookup helper columns.

inverse.reason: Derived columns are dropped per policy; the projection is one-way and not reversible.

entries.name: split_by_discriminator
entries.purpose: Split one frame into per-discriminator frames while recording mapping and optional row-order metadata.

inverse.reason: Strict roundtrip depends on invertible naming, uniform split schemas, and optional row-order metadata.

entries.name: merge_by_discriminator
entries.purpose: Merge per-discriminator frames back into one table by deriving discriminator values from configured frame names.

inverse.reason: Strict roundtrip depends on invertible naming, uniform split schemas, and optional row-order metadata.

entries.name: extract_frame
entries.purpose: Derive a named frame from one source frame by selecting columns, filtering rows with bounded predicates, renaming columns, adding constants, and sorting.

inverse.reason: Projection and filtering can intentionally discard rows or columns; recomposition belongs to dedicated view recomposition steps.

entries.name: pivot_frame
entries.purpose: Derive a named display frame by pivoting long-form rows into configured index columns and bounded value columns.

drops: long-form row multiplicity when aggregating duplicate cells

inverse.reason: Pivoting produces a display view and can aggregate duplicate cells; canonical recomposition belongs to dedicated roundtrip transforms.

entries.name: join_frames
entries.purpose: Derive a named view frame by joining two explicit input frames with configured keys, join mode, projections, and collision policy.

inverse.reason: Join views are materialized projections for display or filtering and do not own canonical recomposition semantics.

entries.name: expand_xref
entries.purpose: Expand matrix/crosstable columns into explicit long-form relation rows.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on drop_empty and explicit column-key policy.

entries.name: contract_xref
entries.purpose: Contract explicit long-form relation rows into a matrix/crosstable frame.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on relation uniqueness and column-key policy.

entries.name: sparse_collapse
entries.purpose: Replace configured default values in editable crosstable cells with a sparse blank placeholder.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on target cells having no pre-existing blank values that conflict with the configured default semantics.

entries.name: sparse_expand
entries.purpose: Replace sparse blank placeholders in editable crosstable cells with the configured default value.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on the sparse blank placeholder representing only the configured default value.

entries.name: normalize_resource_overrides
entries.purpose: Normalize long-form resource override tuples according to an explicit default-context and empty-value policy.

inverse.reason: Normalization may omit empty overrides or collapse overrides equal to defaults according to policy.

entries.name: decode_cell_values
entries.purpose: Decode compact cell values into explicit code or token rows.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on drop_empty, mode, delimiter, and canonical ordering.

entries.name: encode_cell_values
entries.purpose: Encode explicit code or token rows into compact cell values.

inverse.reason: Losslessness depends on grouping, ordering, and codec mode.

entries.name: expand_compact_multiaxis
entries.purpose: Compose xref expansion and cell-value decoding for compact multi-axis matrices.

inverse.reason: Composite is lossless only when wrapped xref and codec policies are lossless.

entries.name: contract_compact_multiaxis
entries.purpose: Compose cell-value encoding and xref contraction for compact multi-axis matrices.

inverse.reason: Composite is lossless only when wrapped xref and codec policies are lossless.

entries.name: add_lookup_helpers
entries.purpose: Join a source frame to a lookup frame and materialize helper columns in an output frame.

key.notes: Preferred YAML-safe single join key spelling.

on.notes: Backward-compatible legacy spelling. In YAML, quote as "on" or prefer key/keys.

helper_value_mode.notes: Controls whether helper columns contain copied values ('values', default) or backend-neutral lookup formulas ('formula'). Formula mode stores LookupFormulaSpec objects as cell values.

writes.reason: Lookup helper provenance is a transient current-run payload registered in meta_registry.yaml.

inverse.reason: Helper columns are derived projections from lookup data and do not own canonical data.
